2606.30572.title: A Multi-task Mixture of Experts Framework for Malware Classification, Packing Detection, and Family Attribution
2606.30572.paper_summary: Malware classification remains a challenging problem due to its inherent heterogeneity, the presence of packed binaries, and the diverse distribution of malware families. Traditional single-model detection mechanisms often fail to generalize across such diverse data, leading to degraded performance, particularly on obfuscated and rare malware samples. In this work, we propose a unified multi-task malware analysis framework based on Mixture of Experts (MoE) architectures. The proposed system evaluates performance across two different input representations, i.e., high-dimensional EMBER feature sets and raw 1D byte arrays extracted from Portable Executable files. It simultaneously performs three critical tasks: malware family classification, packed versus unpacked detection, and malware versus benign identification. By decomposing the problem into specialized expert networks and employing adaptive gating mechanisms, the model enables effective task-specific learning while maintaining overall scalability. We investigate multiple architectural variants, including Homogeneous MoE, Heterogeneous MoE, and Multi-Gate MoE (MMoE). Performance is evaluated in both standard and adversarial settings using original and mutated samples. The obtained results demonstrate that the Multi-Gate MoE model achieves the best performance, reaching a combined detection rate of 0.9744 with only $2.56\%$ failure rate. Moreover, this configuration exhibits improved robustness under mutation-induced distribution shifts. Our findings highlight the effectiveness of expert specialization and task-specific routing in handling complex malware distributions, making the proposed framework a promising direction for scalable and resilient malware detection systems.
2606.30572.paper_summary_zh: 惡意軟體分類仍然是一個具有挑戰性的問題，這是由於其固有的異質性、打包二進位檔的存在以及惡意軟體家族的多樣分佈。傳統的單一模型檢測機制往往無法在如此多樣的數據中進行泛化，導致性能下降，特別是在模糊和稀有的惡意軟體樣本上。在這項工作中，我們提出了一個基於專家混合（MoE）架構的統一多任務惡意軟體分析框架。所提出的系統在兩種不同的輸入表示上評估性能，即高維度的EMBER特徵集和從可攜式執行檔中提取的原始一維位元組數組。它同時執行三個關鍵任務：惡意軟體家族分類、打包與未打包檢測，以及惡意軟體與良性軟體識別。通過將問題分解為專門的專家網絡並採用自適應閘控機制，該模型實現了有效的任務特定學習，同時保持整體可擴展性。我們研究了多種架構變體，包括同質MoE、異質MoE和多閘MoE（MMoE）。在標準和對抗性環境中使用原始和變異樣本評估性能。獲得的結果顯示，多閘MoE模型達到了最佳性能，檢測率達到0.9744，失敗率僅為$2.56\%$。此外，這種配置在突變引起的分佈變化下顯示出更好的穩健性。我們的研究結果突顯了專家專業化和任務特定路由在處理複雜惡意軟體分佈中的有效性，使所提出的框架成為可擴展和具韌性的惡意軟體檢測系統的一個有前景的方向。
2606.30572.author: Jithin S. et.al.
2606.30572.authors: Jithin S., Roshin Sleeba C., Anvin Mariya P. B., Asmitha K. A., Vinod P., Serena Nicolazzo, Antonino Nocera
